assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states 'Classification: CODE CHANGE REQUEST' and 'triggers sub-task creation (see subtask-30001.md)'. subtask-30001.md exists. report.md Review Feedback Classification table shows comment 30001 as 'code change request' with Sub-task 'Yes'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states 'Classification: SUGGESTION'. The Suggestive language analysis section specifically identifies: '"should also add" -- the word "also" frames this as an additional enhancement', '"would help" -- conditional/advisory phrasing', and '"Something like:" -- explicitly frames the code example as illustrative rather than prescriptive'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states 'Classification: NIT' and 'no sub-task created'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md table shows comment 30003 as 'nit' with Sub-task 'No'.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states 'Classification: QUESTION' and 'no sub-task created'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md table shows comment 30004 as 'question' with Sub-task 'No'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1: 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 11), '## Implementation Notes' (line 14), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 21). Only one sub-task exists (subtask-30001.md).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section (line 26) with the URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 27).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section (line 29) with Comment ID 30001, Reviewer reviewer-a, File path, and the full original comment text: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-30001.md), and a report (report.md). No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report and classifications are purely analytical artifacts.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md Review Feedback Classification table lists all 4 comments with their classifications. The report header shows '4 review comments classified'.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no merge commands, no merge actions, and no indication of auto-merging. The overall result is 'WARN' with the note that '1 code change request from reviewer feedback requires a sub-task'. The report is purely informational.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file); no existing tests modified or deleted |' in the summary table, and a dedicated section: 'Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE' stating 'Only new test file added (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`). No existing test files modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full 'Convention upgrade eligibility evaluation' subsection that checks CONVENTIONS.md availability, existing migration patterns, and Key Conventions section. report.md Style/Conventions section contains 'Convention Upgrade Evaluation' paragraph specifically for comment 30002, stating 'Convention analysis found no documented or demonstrated project convention requiring indexes on nullable filter columns'.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. review-30002.md concludes: 'No available project convention backs upgrading this suggestion to a code change request' and 'Classification: suggestion -- no sub-task created.' report.md table shows comment 30002 with Sub-task 'No -- performance optimization recommendation, no backing convention for upgrade'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md states: 'Eval Quality -- N/A' and 'No eval result reviews exist in the PR. No reviews match the 3-criteria detection heuristic (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals).' The Test Quality row in the summary table shows 'Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews exist in the PR'.
